skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Use the inference.sh belt CLI to search the web, extract page content, and get AI-assisted answers from Tavily and Exa.
skill.summary: Search the web and extract page content through Tavily and Exa using the belt CLI.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and network findings are mostly true positives because the skill authorizes Bash(belt *) and documents belt CLI calls to Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and inference.sh resources. The weak-cryptography blocker findings appear to be false positives in prose, table headers, and documentation links; no cryptographic algorithm use or prompt-injection attempt was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash(belt *) and documents many belt commands. This is legitimate for a web-search integration, but it lets the assistant run an external CLI with user-controlled search queries and extraction inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit and repeated, and the allowed tool scope confirms it can be invoked. The intent is functional rather than malicious, so the risk is medium instead of high.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Search and Extraction
medium_findings.description: The skill sends searches, URLs, and extracted page content to external providers through inference.sh, Tavily, Exa, and OpenRouter workflows. Users could expose sensitive queries or private URLs if they use it carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly describes web search and URL extraction over external services. No evidence shows covert exfiltration, but the data-sharing behavior is inherent to the skill.

low_findings.title: External Package Installation Instructions
low_findings.description: The documentation asks users to install CLI-related skills with npx commands. These commands are not auto-executed by the skill, but they depend on external packages and should be reviewed before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are visible documentation examples, not hidden execution logic. They still represent a supply-chain consideration for users who run them.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak-cryptography hits do not identify cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain metadata, Markdown tables, or documentation links, with no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no cryptographic API or algorithm name. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Belt CLI Permission
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed tool scope permits any belt subcommand. This is broader than a single fixed search command and depends on the installed belt CLI behavior.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wildcard permission is directly present in the skill frontmatter. It is not malicious by itself, but it expands the executable surface.

dangerous_patterns.title: User Input Passed to External Search Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples pass query, question, url, and urls values to external apps. This is expected behavior, but private or regulated data could leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow to external apps is shown directly in command examples. The examples use benign placeholder inputs, so the concern is privacy exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: AI assistants often need current sources and clean page text. This skill connects Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows to Tavily and Exa search tools through the inference.sh belt CLI.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily Search Assistant for AI-powered web search with sources.
Runs Tavily Extract to collect text and images from listed URLs.
Runs Exa Search to find relevant web pages for a query.
Runs Exa Answer to request direct factual answers.
Runs Exa Extract to extract and analyze page content.
Shows workflows that combine search results with Claude model analysis.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI and any needed provider credentials.
Sends queries, URLs, and extracted content to external services.
Search freshness and coverage depend on Tavily, Exa, and inference.sh availability.
Does not validate source accuracy beyond returning provider results.

use_cases.title: Research Current Topics
use_cases.description: Collect recent sources, summaries, and links before writing an answer or brief.

use_cases.title: Prepare RAG Inputs
use_cases.description: Find and extract web pages that can seed retrieval-augmented generation workflows.

use_cases.title: Check Claims With Sources
use_cases.description: Search for supporting evidence and compare answers against source pages.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for recent information about [topic]. Return the key findings and cite the most relevant sources.

prompt_templates.title: Extract From URLs
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract readable content from these URLs: [urls]. Summarize the main points and note any missing or inaccessible pages.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Search Providers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily and Exa to research [topic]. Compare the best sources, identify disagreements, and explain which evidence is strongest.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic], extract the top source pages, and prepare a structured research brief suitable for a RAG pipeline.

output_examples.input: Search for best practices for building AI agents.
output_examples.output: A concise research summary with source links, recurring recommendations, and notes about source quality.

output_examples.input: Extract content from two article URLs.
output_examples.output: Clean article text summaries, image references when available, and a short note for any page that could not be extracted.

output_examples.input: Find evidence for a product market claim.
output_examples.output: A source-backed comparison of supporting and conflicting evidence, with recommended next checks.

best_practices: Use specific search queries with dates, product names, or source types when freshness matters.
Avoid sending private URLs, secrets, customer data, or unreleased plans to search providers.
Cross-check important claims with more than one source before using results.
anti_patterns: Do not treat search answers as verified facts without reading the cited sources.
Do not extract pages that require authorization unless you have permission.
Do not use broad queries when a precise research question is available.

faq.question: Which providers does this skill use?
faq.answer: It documents Tavily and Exa apps through the inference.sh belt CLI.

faq.question: Does it require installation?
faq.answer: Yes. The belt CLI must be installed and authenticated before the commands can run.

faq.question: Can it extract page content?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Tavily Extract and Exa Extract examples for URL content extraction.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Queries, URLs, and extracted content may be sent to external services.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code support.

faq.question: Does it guarantee accurate search results?
faq.answer: No. Results depend on external providers and should be checked against original sources.
